tools/workbench-browser-events-fixtures..boundary: no Playwright, withRuntime, or Workbench API writeback imports

tools/workbench-browser-events-runtime.m.domain: mounted Next runtime setup, page opening, readouts, event ledger, and legacy-shell assertions
tools/workbench-browser-events-runtime.m.boundary: only extracted module importing withRuntime and WORKBENCH_MOUNT_PREFIX

tools/workbench-browser-events-default-s.domain: success, failure, provider-health, scheduler-dispatch, approved-mock, and guarded next-action UI scenarios

tools/workbench-browser-events-lifecycle.domain: agent lifecycle timeout, cleanup click, and cleanup loop UI scenarios

tools/workbench-browser-events-projected.domain: projected mock/real shard, terminal readout, autonomous loop, mobile projection, and durable projection UI scenarios

scenario_comparison.order_note: The required_scenarios contract order is unchanged. The actual execution order preserves the pre-extraction script behavior, where mobile_projection runs before latest_durable_global_goal_lifecycle_projection.

focused_gate.post_refactor_contract_assertion: Root scenario group mapping and actual scenarioResults are asserted against required_scenarios after browser execution.

deepseek_blocker_remediation.root_mount_prefix_boundary: Root no longer imports WORKBENCH_MOUNT_PREFIX directly; API writeback URLs are built through workbenchApiUrl exported by the runtime module.
deepseek_blocker_remediation.scenario_group_mapping: Root now declares SCENARIO_GROUPS with explicit scenario-name coverage and fails after execution if any required scenario is missing or duplicated.
deepseek_blocker_remediation.hardcoded_projection_fields: Mobile and durable projection scenarios now record page readouts, locator counts, body-derived rendered checks, dimensions, and explicitly named expected_* values instead of presenting hardcoded projection counters as page evidence.
